meta.title: WDSF Standard Syllabus — figure seed data for Weave Steps
meta.purpose: Source data for an agent to generate the default global FigureDocs (application-scoped library) per docs/PLAN.md D30 / Q-LIBSEED. Each entry maps to one global FigureDoc (figureType x dance).
meta.source: WDSF Syllabus (World DanceSport Federation), Standard division.
meta.extractedFields: figure name, dance, WDSF start/finish/timing/notes, cross-references.
meta.notProvidedBySource: Per-count footwork (HT/T/TH/H/heel_pull), sway, rise, turn amount and alignment timelines are NOT in the WDSF syllabus document — those live in the paid technique books (Laird/ISTD). They are left as an empty `attributes` array to be populated by the accuracy-validated content workstream (PLAN §9: best-effort now, refine with testers).
meta.planDiscrepancy: PLAN.md D30/Q-LIBSEED names ISTD as the seed source; this file is WDSF (per the request). The two syllabi overlap heavily in figure names; if ISTD is the system of record, treat this as a cross-reference / starting enumeration.
meta.techniqueBooks: Figure charts, timing, and levels refined against the official WDSF Technique Books (2nd ed., May 2013; Waltz / Viennese Waltz / Tango / Slow Foxtrot / Quickstep), owner-provided copies. wdsfLevel = the book's choreographic level (Bronze/Silver/Gold).

figureIdentity.figureType: stable cross-dance family id (slug). Same family across dances shares it (e.g. `feather` / `natural-turn`) so a note can target the whole family.
figureIdentity.name: display name
figureIdentity.entryAlignment: per-figure: { qualifier, direction } — see alignment vocab

attributeEnvelope.kind: one of the attribute kinds below (or a user-defined kind)
attributeEnvelope.count: float, relative to figure start, modulo the dance phrase; fractions render e=.25, &=.5, a=.75, ia=.125, ai=.375 (PLAN §2.5, Q-D3)

turn.note: PLAN states the range 'eighth_L … half_R / none'; values enumerated here best-effort

captureLevels.customKinds: User-defined attribute kinds are supported (PLAN D22); the 5 above are the v1 standard kinds.

NOTE - Sway. If the dancers will apply the Sway is possible to create two particular interpretation of the Progressive Chasse. (Case 1) Step/Action 1-4 — Type of Sway (Man): To R (Releasing) e/o 1; To R (Cosmetic); Straight; Slightly to L (Cosmetic). Type of Sway (Lady): To L (Releasing) e/o 1; To L (Cosmetic); Straight; Slightly to R (Cosmetic). (Case 2) Step/Action 1-4 — Type of Sway (Man): To L (Cosmetic) e/o 1; Straight; Straight; To R (Cosmetic). Type of Sway (Lady): To R (Cosmetic) e/o 1; Straight; Straight; To L (Cosmetic).
NOTE - Rotation: (1) Man and Lady will continue to turn hips also on step 3.
NOTE - Foot Placement/Couple position: may start with LF fwd in CBMP in Wing Position.
NOTE - Quantity of Turn: may turn up to 1/2 to L.
Charted from WDSF Technique Book — Waltz (2nd ed., May 2013), fig. 5, p. 53.

NOTE - Quantity of Turn: may be underturned to end backing DC against LOD or overturned to end
NOTE - Foot Placement: (1) during step 4, due to the Pivoting Action, the Lady has to swing her free leg (L) into the next position.

NOTE - Alignment-Direction: (1) in step 4 the final alignment is Backing LOD, but for better fluidity of movement it is necessary to stop the turn a bit earlier to reach this alignment at the beginning of the next step.

NOTE - Quantity of turn: may be underturned to end backing DC against LOD (3/8 to R on step 4, 1/4 to R between 5 and 6). May be overturned to end almost backing LOD (1/2 to R on step 4, just under 1/2 to R between 5 and 6).
NOTE - Type of Sway: (1) if the Natural Spin Turn is preceding a Turning Lock to Right the Sway will be exactly the opposite due to the overturned action (Man to R and Lady to L).

TAP after OPEN FINISH - Foot Placement (Man), Prec. Step: RF fwd in CBMP | Recover LF; Foot Placement (Lady), Prec. Step: LF bwd in CBMP | Recover RF
Note (TAP after OPEN FINISH) - Quantity of Turn: due to a slight turn to R on the last step of the Open Finish, the Man will make a slight turn to L on the RF during the Tap.
